test_cases:
[
  {
    "testCaseId": "TC_DL_001",
    "title": "Verify peak downlink throughput measurement under excellent radio conditions with single UE in isolated cell",
    "description": "Measure and verify the maximum achievable downlink user data throughput with a stationary UE under excellent radio conditions inside a single isolated cell with no inter-cell interference.",
    "preconditions": [
      "Single isolated cell configured with all other cells turned off",
      "UE placed at cell center close to radiated gNB antenna",
      "UE is stationary and under excellent radio conditions as per SINR thresholds",
      "Application server connected to core emulator with sufficient transport capacity",
      "UE powered on and attached to the network"
    ],
    "testSteps": [
      "Configure the test setup as a single cell scenario with no inter-cell interference",
      "Place the UE under excellent radio conditions using SINR-based thresholds (clause 4.6)",
      "Start downlink full-buffer UDP data transmission from the application server to UE",
      "Monitor and verify stable utilization of highest downlink MCS, transport block size, and MIMO rank at the UE",
      "Measure and log radio parameters (RSRP, RSRQ, CQI, PDSCH SINR), PDSCH BLER, throughput (L1 and Application layers), downlink transmission mode, channel utilization (PRBs, slots) at UE",
      "Measure and log transmitted downlink throughput at application server",
      "Continue test for at least 3 minutes ensuring throughput stability and unchanged UE location",
      "Stop data transmission and capture logs"
    ],
    "expectedResults": [
      "Achieved downlink throughput corresponds to peak user data throughput",
      "Stable highest possible downlink MCS, transport block size, and MIMO rank utilization observed",
      "Minimum downlink BLER observed",
      "No throughput degradation during 3 minutes",
      "Logged KPIs match expected values for excellent radio conditions"
    ],
    "postconditions": [
      "UE remains attached and can be powered off or set to airplane mode to empty buffers"
    ],
    "commandsUsed": [
      "Connect via SSH using `admin@<IP>` to access CLI if needed for configuration",
      "Use CLI or management interface to configure cell and UE placement",
      "Use application server interface to start/stop data transmission"
    ],
    "verificationMethods": [
      "Analyze logs of radio parameters and throughput",
      "Check SINR, MCS, BLER stability graphs over test duration",
      "Confirm application and L1 layer throughput consistency"
    ],
    "category": ["POSITIVE TESTING", "PERFORMANCE TESTING", "INTEGRATION TESTING"]
  },
  {
    "testCaseId": "TC_DL_002",
    "title": "Verify test behavior when UE is placed beyond minimum coupling loss resulting in degraded radio conditions",
    "description": "Test the impact on downlink peak throughput measurement when the UE is placed such that minimum coupling loss is exceeded, leading to non-excellent radio conditions.",
    "preconditions": [
      "Single isolated cell active with all others off",
      "UE positioned beyond specified minimum coupling loss threshold",
      "UE powered on and attached to network"
    ],
    "testSteps": [
      "Place UE in cell but beyond minimum coupling loss limit as defined in clause 4.6",
      "Start downlink full-buffer UDP data transmission from application server",
      "Monitor radio parameters and throughput at UE",
      "Record the impact on MCS, MIMO rank, BLER, and throughput",
      "Stop data transmission and capture logs"
    ],
    "expectedResults": [
      "Degraded SINR values observed below excellent threshold",
      "Reduced downlink MCS, transport block size, and MIMO rank utilization",
      "Increased downlink BLER compared to excellent conditions",
      "Reduced peak throughput compared to baseline",
      "Logs reflect lower spectral efficiency"
    ],
    "postconditions": [
      "UE can be repositioned or reset for further testing"
    ],
    "commandsUsed": [
      "Generic positioning and configuration commands or manual UE placement",
      "Application server data transmission start/stop commands"
    ],
    "verificationMethods": [
      "Compare throughput and radio parameters logs against baseline excellent condition test",
      "Check SINR and BLER degradation",
      "Calculate spectral efficiency reduction"
    ],
    "category": ["NEGATIVE TESTING", "EDGE CASES", "PERFORMANCE TESTING"]
  },
  {
    "testCaseId": "TC_DL_003",
    "title": "Verify downlink peak throughput measurement with UE emulated under variable radio conditions using fading generator",
    "description": "Use a UE emulator and variable attenuator/fading generator to emulate excellent and varying radio conditions, measuring downlink peak throughput stability and KPIs.",
    "preconditions": [
      "Lab environment with UE emulator connected via cable",
      "Variable attenuator/fading generator inserted between O-RU and UE emulator antenna connectors",
      "Application server connected with sufficient capacity",
      "Test environment configured for excellent radio conditions"
    ],
    "testSteps": [
      "Configure UE emulator under cable connection",
      "Adjust variable attenuator/fading generator to achieve excellent SINR conditions",
      "Start downlink full-buffer UDP data transmission from application server",
      "Verify stable utilization of highest downlink MCS, transport block size, and MIMO rank",
      "Gradually vary attenuation to simulate borderline radio conditions and observe impact",
      "Stop data transmission and capture logs"
    ],
    "expectedResults": [
      "Under excellent conditions, peak throughput and KPIs stable as expected",
      "As attenuation increases, MCS, MIMO rank and throughput reduce accordingly",
      "BLER increases with degraded radio conditions",
      "Logs capture KPIs accurately reflecting radio condition changes"
    ],
    "postconditions": [
      "Reset attenuation to baseline",
      "UE emulator remains connected"
    ],
    "commandsUsed": [
      "Generic commands for UE emulator configuration",
      "Application server commands for data transmission control"
    ],
    "verificationMethods": [
      "Analyze KPI logs over attenuation changes",
      "Plot throughput and radio parameters vs time",
      "Confirm correlation between SINR and throughput changes"
    ],
    "category": ["POSITIVE TESTING", "EDGE CASES", "PERFORMANCE TESTING"]
  },
  {
    "testCaseId": "TC_DL_004",
    "title": "Verify test recovery when UE is powered off or set to airplane mode to empty buffers before test start",
    "description": "Validate that powering off or setting the UE to airplane mode correctly empties buffers and prepares the UE for downlink throughput test.",
    "preconditions": [
      "UE attached to network and previously active",
      "Buffers may contain data from prior transmissions"
    ],
    "testSteps": [
      "Power off the UE or set to airplane mode",
      "Wait sufficient time to ensure buffers are emptied",
      "Power on UE and attach to network again",
      "Start downlink full-buffer UDP data transmission",
      "Verify no residual data in buffers affects throughput measurement"
    ],
    "expectedResults": [
      "Buffers are emptied successfully before test start",
      "Downlink throughput measurement starts with clean state",
      "No anomalies or inflated throughput values due to residual buffer data"
    ],
    "postconditions": [
      "UE attached and ready for subsequent tests"
    ],
    "commandsUsed": [
      "Generic UE power off/on or airplane mode commands",
      "Connect via SSH `admin@<IP>` if CLI control available",
      "Application server commands for data transmission"
    ],
    "verificationMethods": [
      "Check logs for buffer clearance confirmation",
      "Verify throughput stability from test start",
      "Confirm no residual data packets transmitted"
    ],
    "category": ["NEGATIVE TESTING", "INTEGRATION TESTING"]
  },
  {
    "testCaseId": "TC_DL_005",
    "title": "Verify downlink peak throughput measurement with TCP full-buffer data transmission",
    "description": "Repeat the downlink peak throughput test using TCP full-buffer data transmission instead of UDP and verify KPIs and throughput stability.",
    "preconditions": [
      "Test setup as per UDP baseline test with single UE under excellent radio conditions",
      "Application server configured to send TCP full-buffer traffic"
    ],
    "testSteps": [
      "Configure application server to start TCP full-buffer downlink data transmission",
      "Ensure UE is attached and under excellent radio conditions",
      "Monitor KPIs including MCS, MIMO rank, BLER, and throughput",
      "Maintain test duration at least 3 minutes with stable throughput",
      "Stop data transmission and capture logs"
    ],
    "expectedResults": [
      "TCP downlink throughput achieves peak values close to UDP baseline",
      "Stable utilization of highest downlink MCS and MIMO rank observed",
      "Low BLER maintained",
      "Throughput graphs over time show stability"
    ],
    "postconditions": [
      "Application server stops TCP data transmission",
      "UE remains attached"
    ],
    "commandsUsed": [
      "Application server commands to start/stop TCP data streams",
      "Generic UE monitoring commands"
    ],
    "verificationMethods": [
      "Compare throughput and KPI logs with UDP test results",
      "Validate TCP transmission stability and error rates"
    ],
    "category": ["POSITIVE TESTING", "PERFORMANCE TESTING"]
  },
  {
    "testCaseId": "TC_DL_006",
    "title": "Verify KPI logging accuracy
